context: Checks whether the agent can conduct a broad but evidence-disciplined assessment without fabricating defects or claiming unavailable integration/native verification.

checklist.name: Project profile
checklist.description: Profiles wrappers/modules, Java/toolchains, Quarkus BOM/extensions, execution models, generated sources, migrations, tests, CI, and production native target.

checklist.name: Evidence classification
checklist.description: Separates confirmed defects, likely risks, design trade-offs, and scanner candidates with confidence and concrete locations.

checklist.name: Risk ranking
checklist.description: Prioritizes security/authz, data/transaction/messaging correctness, execution-model issues, API compatibility, and native risk before cosmetic cleanup.

checklist.name: Contract capture
checklist.description: Lists HTTP/event/schema/transaction/idempotency/security/observability/native contracts and unchanged behavior to protect.

checklist.name: Minimal target shape
checklist.description: Recommends only boundaries and simplifications supported by actual complexity, avoiding generic clean-architecture layers.

checklist.name: Staged batches
checklist.description: Creates small dependency-aware batches with evidence, scope, rollback, and acceptance criteria.

checklist.name: Verification per batch
checklist.description: Attaches focused tests and wrapper commands to every batch, then widens to module/repository gates.

checklist.name: Honest limitations
checklist.description: States unit tests that were run and identifies Docker/Dev Services/native gates as blocked or delegated, without calling them passed.

checklist.name: Quarkus semantics
checklist.description: Checks REST dispatch, CDI augmentation/removal, transaction/messaging acknowledgement, config phases, OIDC, telemetry, migrations, and native reachability as applicable.

checklist.name: Residual risk
checklist.description: Names unverified integration/native behavior, required environment, owner, and evidence needed to close each gap.
